notes: This is a READ-ONLY HTTP layer (Layer 2) for the brand detail page's OKR tab. It performs 7 batched queries totaling 5 legacy doctypes (Objective, Key Result, VT Project, KPI Definition, KPI Entry) + User + VT Brand (both unaffected). Key migration points: (1) Objective → VT Item node_type='OKR' with parent_vt_item=brand; status field renamed to health_status; objective_owner→owner_user. (2) Key Result → child table rows on OKR node (fieldname='key_results', child doctype 'VT Item Key Result'); all data fields preserved. (3) VT Project remains VT Project doctype but its objective Link now points to VT Item (OKR node). (4) KPI Definition → VT Item node_type='KPI' with parent_vt_item pointing to OKR node; kpi_name, unit, frequency, target_value fields preserved. (5) KPI Entry → child table rows on KPI node (fieldname='kpi_entries', child doctype 'VT Item KPI Entry'); date and value fields preserved. The API calls two external functions: brand_execution() from portal_brands (which itself reads VT Project, VT Sprint, VT Task—all already migrated per the user spec) and aggregate_kr_progress() from objective.py (a pure function, unaffected). All permission checks use Frappe's native has_permission() API which operates on doctype names and remain valid after migration. Test class TestBrandOkrEndpoint seeded legacy docs directly (Objective, Key Result, VT Project, KPI Definition, KPI Entry) and must be updated to use VT Item API instead; pure-function tests (TestBrandOkrGrouping, TestKpiHelpers, TestBrandOkrProjectsBridge) are unaffected by migration.

writes_creates.tree_equivalent: Key Result child rows are now created as children in the 'key_results' table on a VT Item node with node_type='OKR'. During creation of a KR via API: doc = frappe.get_doc({'doctype': 'VT Item', 'node_type': 'OKR', 'parent_vt_item': <parent_okr_name>, 'key_results': [{'metric': ..., 'target_value': ..., 'current_value': ..., 'unit': ..., 'confidence': ...}]}) then doc.insert(). Alternatively, if the OKR node already exists, append a child row to the existing node's key_results table via frappe.db.sql or row manipulation then doc.save(). The Objective link becomes the OKR node's name; child table rows use 'name' as idempotent identifier (idx fields preserved).

notes: This API layer is a thin whitelist wrapper for Key Result CRUD with no service calls. The module enforces allow-list guards against mass-assignment (KEY_RESULT_EDITABLE_FIELDS excludes progress_percent which is controller-computed). Key Results remain standalone in the legacy Objective doctype (additive P1 migration); they do NOT yet become child table rows on OKR VT Item nodes — the current code still works with legacy Objective/Key Result doctypes. Migration path: (1) Keep legacy doctypes readable for backward-compat. (2) Update create_key_result to write to both legacy Key Result AND append to OKR node's key_results child table, or pivot to pure VT Item tree writes. (3) Update get_key_result/update_key_result to read/write from child table rows. Objective doctype does NOT have an API endpoint (uses Frappe native quick entry per spec 2026-06-07-objective-native-quick-entry-design.html). The test file uses legacy doctype inserts directly but can continue as-is since it exercises the API contract, not internal structure. Field renames noted in spec (objective_owner → owner_user, status → health_status) apply to Objective-as-OKR-node; Key Result field names are preserved. No pdca_phase/kanban_status rules apply to Key Results (they are measurements, not tasks).

notes: CRITICAL MIGRATION NOTES: (1) Portal_brands.py is a read-only brand/reporting API with no direct service calls. All legacy doctype queries (VT Project, VT Sprint, VT Task) must migrate to vt_item_tree helpers. (2) Field renames: project_owner->owner_user, Project.status->health_status, Sprint.status->sprint_state. VT Sprint.sprint_title->VT Item.title. VT Project.objective link becomes parent_vt_item when node is child of OKR; for reporting it reads parent_vt_item (child table or ancestor). (3) Tree navigation: VT Project.brand remains as-is (independent field on VT Item). Parent-child links (Task.project, Sprint.project) become parent_vt_item with node_type filtering. (4) The test file (test_portal_brands.py) uses legacy doctypes directly (_mk_project, _mk_task, _mk_sprint builders); tests MUST be rewritten to use VT Item node_type="Project"/"Sprint"/"Task" structure. Tests reference fields like estimated_minutes, kanban_status, pdca_phase which map 1:1. (5) brand_execution() reads VT Project.percent_done (computed field) and VT Project.objective (Link to OKR) — both translate to vt_item tree reads with parent_vt_item as the "objective" reference. (6) No legacy doctype creates in the API itself (only get_all/get_doc reads + delete checks); test helpers that create must migrate. (7) VT Brand doctype itself is unchanged and independent of the VT Item tree.

notes: This API layer (board_mutations.py) is a thin HTTP wrapper that delegates all state-machine enforcement to the VT Task controller. It handles 5 whitelist endpoints: move_task (drag to column), create_task (quick-add from column), update_task (modal save multi-field), patch_task (inline-edit single field), get_task (hydrate modal), bulk_assign (batch assign). AUTHZ is project-level via _assert_project_access (admin/leader/member check). WRITES bypass row-level permissions via ignore_permissions=True because project membership IS the board's authorization contract. KEY FIELD MAPPING FOR MIGRATION: (1) VT Task.project Link -> VT Item.parent_vt_item (reference to Project node, not tree parent). (2) Task.assigned_to -> Task node.assigned_to (no rename). (3) Kanban_status derivation: NEVER set directly except 'Blocked' flag; controller's _sync_kanban_status() derives it from pdca_phase via PDCA_KANBAN_MAP. (4) Completion: task done = pdca_phase=='DONE', not set via completion_date in create/move. (5) No legacy Objective/KR/KPI/Sprint doctype references in this API — all Task-scoped. (6) Team member validation: must check child-table rows on Project node (not separate Project Team Member doctype). (7) Dependencies/schedule_entries remain as child rows on Task node. (8) Recurring parent link preserved as recurring_parent (plain Link, not tree parent). The service layer (task/services/*) is already migrated to VT Item; if this API called services, those calls are fine as-is. PDCA_KANBAN_MAP and VALID_PDCA_TRANSITIONS state machine is the single source of truth for legality — all moves validated there.

notes: This is a READ-ONLY dashboard API with 6 main endpoints: me_progress(), my_projects(), project_detail(), project_board(), project_sprints(), and schedule_agenda(). It also includes personal_stats(), daily_completions(), hours_summary() (folded from deleted my_dashboard page) and team_overview(), team_tab_state() (folded from deleted leader_dashboard page). All reads are against VT Task, VT Project, VT Sprint, VT Brand, Project Team Member (child table), Project Milestone (child table), and Vernon Meeting (optional). CRITICAL: The code uses kanban_status derived from pdca_phase via PDCA_KANBAN_MAP imported from vt_task.py—kanban_status is NOT stored in VT Item (post-migration), it's computed. pdca_phase field name is preserved. Field mapping: Objective status→health_status, Project status→health_status (preserved in reads), Sprint status→sprint_state. The API never calls any other services. No creates/writes. Test file uses legacy VT Task/VT Project/VT Sprint doctypes with fields like 'pdca_phase', 'kanban_status', 'base_points', 'completion_date', 'earned_points', 'revision_count', 'actual_minutes', 'estimated_minutes', 'deadline', 'priority', 'start_date', 'assigned_to', 'project', 'sprint', 'risk_flag'. Tests verify read-only behavior, access control (via _assert_project_access), overdue/due_soon/due_today flags (computed server-side), and count accuracy. No test mocks or services.

notes: CRITICAL MIGRATION ISSUES: (1) VT Task has assigned_to field; VT Item has owner_user — all filter/read/write ops must rename. (2) VT Task.pdca_phase='DONE' maps to VT Item.pdca_phase='CLOSED' — filter/comparison logic must be updated. (3) VT Task has project and sprint Link fields pointing to separate doctypes; VT Item has only parent_vt_item — task-to-project resolution requires tree walk (vt_item_tree.project_of(task_node)) or parent chain walk. (4) kanban_status on VT Item is DERIVED from pdca_phase via PDCA_KANBAN_MAP in the controller — do NOT set kanban_status directly except for \"Blocked\"; test carefully after migration. (5) list() and search() filter by \"assigned_to\"; in VT Item universe these map to owner_user. (6) my_work.py does NOT call any migrated services; all reads/writes are direct frappe.get_all/db.sql/db.get_value/db.set_value. (7) Task Dependency and Task Schedule Entry are preserved as child tables on VT Item — queries remain in-structure (child_table_rows) but must change from `tabTask Dependency` JOINs to child iteration. (8) Comment doctype is NOT migrated; queries for Comments by reference_doctype/reference_name remain as-is. (9) Tests create VT Task and VT Project legacy docs directly — migration requires updating fixture creation to use VT Item with node_type="Task"/"Project"/"Sprint" and renaming assigned_to→owner_user. (10) The get_my_day(), get_what_to_do_today(), get_my_blocked_tasks() functions use raw SQL with JOINs on tabVT Task and tabTask Dependency — these must be rewritten to use vt_item_tree helpers and child_table_rows iteration. (11) start_task() and submit_for_review() use frappe.db.set_value to update pdca_phase and kanban_status together; post-migration must verify PDCA_KANBAN_MAP behavior or fetch doc, update, and save.

notes: SMALL SCOPE: Pure task mutation API — no service calls. All changes are straightforward 1:1 field renames (assigned_to→owner_user) and doctype swaps (VT Task→VT Item). Key points: (1) _check_access() reads task existence and assigned_to field; (2) complete() sets pdca_phase='DONE', kanban_status='Done', and completion_date. After migration, remove kanban_status assignment since PDCA_KANBAN_MAP sync in VT Item controller handles it. (3) log_progress() increments actual_minutes and creates Comment with reference_doctype='VT Task'; change to 'VT Item'. (4) snooze() updates deadline. (5) Test fixture creates VT Project and VT Task; both will need to become VT Item nodes. Test constants: TASK_DOCTYPE='VT Task' hardcoded in line 5, must change to 'VT Item'. Test setup creates Project and Task via frappe.get_doc() — convert to VT Item with node_type. All field names in task payloads stay the same (title, deadline, weight, pdca_phase, kanban_status, actual_minutes, completion_date). No recurring_parent, Task Point Log, or complex tree queries used here.

notes: onboarding.py is HTTP API layer with 4 endpoints: get_onboarding_state (derives completion per-user), dismiss_onboarding (sets user default), load_demo (delegates to setup/demo_data.load), clear_demo (delegates to setup/demo_data.clear). The API itself does NOT create/read legacy doctypes directly EXCEPT for VT Brand (which is NOT migrated to tree) and VT Project/VT Task/VT Sprint reads via helper functions. Main migration effort is in setup/demo_data.py: it creates Project/Sprint/Task docs. The _user_project_names() function has TWO queries: (1) project_owner/project_leader on VT Project (trivial tree conversion), (2) Project Team Member lookup which is NOT a tree operation — team_members is a child table on VT Project legacy doctype and is NOT preserved in VT Item tree structure. DECISION NEEDED: does onboarding step "tambah_tim" still apply? If so, either keep querying legacy Project Team Member, or query the VT Item project node's team_members child table if that table is preserved. All other functions (_is_complete, get_onboarding_state, dismiss_onboarding, clear_demo) are framework/settings layer and do not directly query doctypes. Test file test_onboarding.py creates test VT Project and VT Task docs in setUp/test methods — these will also need to be converted to VT Item node creates. CRITICAL: demo_data.py refs storage mechanism must be updated to track VT Item node names instead of legacy doctype names (or dual-track during transition).

notes: CRITICAL: The portal API is the HTTP layer serving the React portal. It does legacy doctype reads/writes on VT Project, VT Task, VT Sprint, and Key Result. The migration maps these to VT Item tree: Project/Sprint/Task/OKR/KPI are node_type values on a single doctype. Key migrations: (1) VT Project.project_leader → VT Item Project.leader_user; VT Project.project_owner → owner_user; VT Project.status → health_status; VT Project.objective (Link to Objective doctype) → parent_vt_item (Link to OKR node). (2) VT Task.sprint (Link to VT Sprint) → parent_vt_item (tree parent); VT Task.assigned_to → owner_user; pdca_phase unchanged. (3) VT Sprint.status → sprint_state; VT Sprint.project (Link) → parent_vt_item (tree parent). (4) Key Result doctype no longer exists; KRs are child-table rows (VT Item Key Result) on OKR nodes. The relink_task_kr() function validates KR existence but does NOT persist (linked_kr column does not exist per schema notes), so validation can check frappe.db.exists('VT Item', {'node_type': 'KR'}) or tree navigation. (5) For task sprinting, bulk_move_tasks() sets sprint Link → must now reparent VT Item Task to target Sprint node (set parent_vt_item). (6) Field renames in EDITABLE_PROJECT_FIELDS and elsewhere: objective stays as a key name but maps to parent_vt_item on write. (7) Kanban: "Blocked" status is Title-cased (not "BLOCKED"). (8) Health/status fields: Project.status → health_status; Sprint.status → sprint_state. (9) The service project_task_grouper is already migrated (uses vt_item_tree). The API itself needs migration in list_projects(), get_project_detail(), bulk_move_tasks(), bulk_reassign(), bulk_phase_shift(), relink_task_kr() validation, create_project(), update_project(), delete_project(), get_project_members()."

notes: CRITICAL: This API is mostly read-only child-table operations and queries. The worksheet_aggregator service (already migrated) handles the main aggregation logic via vt_item_tree foundation helpers. Portal_worksheet.py's direct database and frappe.get_doc() calls need migration: (1) All 'VT Task' doctype reads → 'VT Item' with node_type='Task'; (2) SQL queries on tabVT Task and tabTask Schedule Entry → Use tree.nodes('Task', ...) + tree.child_table_rows(node, 'schedule_entries') pattern. (3) Field rename: assigned_to → owner_user (already present in vt_item.json at line 25). (4) No creates of Task/Project/Sprint (just appending schedule_entries child rows, which stay as-is). (5) Line 194: assigned_to fallback → use owner_user; owner_user on Task Schedule Entry row already exists in schema. (6) PDCA phase filtering: _OPEN_PHASES is correct for Tasks; note that vt_item.json shows pdca_phase includes 'CLOSED' terminal (line 35). (7) Kanban/points logic: unchanged (all fields preserved). (8) Line 14 _SCHEDULE_ENTRY_DOCTYPE hardcoded but only for reference—actual writes/reads operate on parent Task's child table. Complexity: M because SQL aggregation (line 214–231) must move to Python post-fetch, but worksheet_aggregator already delegates to tree helpers; this API mainly orchestrates framing/validation.

notes: This API layer is read-only (get_burndown endpoint). Migration path: (1) Change frappe.db.exists(\"VT Sprint\", sprint_id) to vt_item_tree.nodes(\"Sprint\", {\"name\": sprint_id}) check; (2) Update frappe.has_permission(\"VT Sprint\", ...) to check \"VT Item\" doctype instead; (3) Replace frappe.db.get_value(\"VT Sprint\", ...) with vt_item_tree.nodes(\"Sprint\", filters={\"name\": sprint_id}) and get the doc; (4) Replace frappe.db.count(\"VT Task\", {\"sprint\": sprint_id}) with vt_item_tree.children(sprint_id, \"Task\") and count results; (5) Replace the kanban_status filter count by passing filters={\"kanban_status\": (\"not in\", [\"Done\"])} to children(). VT Sprint.project link becomes parent_vt_item on Sprint node. Field burndown_actual_json is preserved on Sprint node_type. Task.sprint FK link is replaced by Task node's parent_vt_item pointing to Sprint node (Tasks are direct children of Sprints in tree). Test file has only 1 test (nonexistent sprint check); expand coverage during migration.
